description:
Context:
    You are a QA engineer preparing a clean workspace for automated regression tests.  
    Inside the home directory a sub-directory already exists:

        /home/user/test_data/

    and it contains one CSV file called **test_results.csv** whose content is shown below.  
    No other files are present in that directory.

        TC_ID,Status
        TC001,PASS
        TC002,FAIL
        TC003,PASS
        TC004,FAIL
        TC005,SKIP
        TC006,FAIL
        TC007,PASS
        TC008,SKIP
        TC009,PASS
        TC010,PASS

    Task:
    1. Extract the identifiers of every failed test case (i.e. every line whose “Status” column is exactly the string **FAIL**, case-sensitive).  
    2. Write those identifiers, and **only** those identifiers, to a brand-new plain-text file located at  

           /home/user/test_data/failed_tests.txt

       Output format requirements for **failed_tests.txt**:
         • Each identifier on its own line with no leading or trailing spaces.  
         • Do **not** include the header line or any other data.  
         • Lines must be sorted in lexicographical order (ascending).  
         • The file must end with a single UNIX newline (LF).  
       The automated grader will fail if any extra characters, blank lines, or spaces appear.

    3. Create an operation log called  

           /home/user/test_data/extract_failures.log

       It must contain **exactly two lines**, in this exact order:

           TIMESTAMP: NOW
           FAILED_COUNT: <N>

       where <N> is the total number of failed cases you found (as written to *failed_tests.txt*).  
       Use the literal word **NOW**—do not substitute the current date/time.  
       Ensure a single LF separates the two lines and the file ends with a final LF.

    Constraints:
    • Accomplish both steps with a single pipeline or compound command issued from the shell (e.g., using grep, awk, cut, sort, tee, etc.).  
    • You do **not** have root privileges; work only inside /home/user.  
    • Do not modify the original *test_results.csv* file.

    Success criteria (checked by automated tests):
      a) /home/user/test_data/failed_tests.txt exists and contains exactly:
             TC002
             TC004
             TC006
         followed by one LF and nothing else.  
      b) /home/user/test_data/extract_failures.log exists and contains exactly:
             TIMESTAMP: NOW
             FAILED_COUNT: 3
         followed by one LF and nothing else.
truth:
Final state expected by the grader:

    1. File: /home/user/test_data/test_results.csv  
       (Already provided before task starts. Content as listed in the task description.)

    2. File the agent must create: /home/user/test_data/failed_tests.txt  
       Exact byte content (including final newline):
           "TC002\nTC004\nTC006\n"

    3. File the agent must create: /home/user/test_data/extract_failures.log  
       Exact byte content (including final newline):
           "TIMESTAMP: NOW\nFAILED_COUNT: 3\n"
name: task_000000_d4f2a584
